content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion taught in high school language curricula frames it as a practice of re-examining one’s own written output to align it with intended communication goals, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to borrow this structured, communicative framing rather than only focusing on logical error correction. This framing treats LLM self-reflection as a process of aligning the model’s output with the implicit communicative intent of the user’s prompt, rather than just correcting factual or logical mistakes.
